summary: Round 3 validation. Checked 19 claims across all 5 targeted items. Found 0 mirages. All round-2 fixes are correctly reflected in the current tech-spec.

verified_claims: top/config.go:14 — config.viewCh chan view.View exists, accessible from any function with *config
top/config_view.go:188-191 — viewSwitchHandler(config *config, c string): saves current, loads from map, sends on viewCh immediately
spec manual patch pattern (save→load→patch CollectExtra/IOAvailable→set config.view→send viewCh) is the correct workaround for viewSwitchHandler's string signature
internal/query/activity.go:14-15 — PgStatActivityDefault embeds '{{.QueryAgeThresh}}'::interval unconditionally, no conditional guard
internal/query/query.go:43 — QueryAgeThresh default is '00:00:00.0', never empty
spec SQL constant (tech-spec line 288) uses unconditional QueryAgeThresh embedding — matches existing convention
internal/stat/stat.go:100-103 — Collector.Reset() exists, clears prevPgStat and currPgStat
internal/stat/stat.go:100-103 — Reset() does not yet clear PID maps (they do not exist yet); spec Task 5 correctly states to extend Reset()
top/stat.go:81-85 — ShowExtra change-detection calls c.ToggleCollectExtra(extra) then continue, does NOT call c.Reset()
top/stat.go:87-94 — c.Reset() is called in view-switch branch (new view on viewCh), not in ShowExtra branch
spec CollectExtra change-detection (parallel to ShowExtra branch, calling c.Reset()) is architecturally correct
internal/stat/stat.go:25-29 — CollectNone=0 CollectDiskstats=1 CollectNetdev=2 CollectFsstats=3 CollectLogtail=4
spec states CollectProcPidStat = 5 — correct next iota value, no collision
internal/view/view.go:24 — View.Filters map[int]*regexp.Regexp{} initialized in all existing views
internal/view/view.go:26 — View.ShowExtra int exists; spec CollectExtra int is a new separate field
top/dialog.go:51 — compound guard '(d > dialogFilter && d <= dialogChangeAge) && name != activity' confirmed; Decision 7 isolation is required
record/record.go:147-165 — filterViews() filters by version and pgss schema only; NotRecordable skip is new addition

q1_view_patching.detail: viewSwitchHandler(config *config, c string) at top/config_view.go:188-191 takes a string, loads view from map, sends on viewCh. The spec's manual load→patch→send pattern is the only viable approach. config.viewCh is accessible from switchViewToProcPidStat via app.config. Pattern is consistent with how toggleSysTables (config_view.go:221-227) and changeQueryAge (config_view.go:259-260) directly manipulate config.view and send on viewCh without going through viewSwitchHandler.

q2_queryagethresh.detail: All three activity query variants embed QueryAgeThresh unconditionally. Default is '00:00:00.0' (never empty). The SQL constant in the spec (line 288) correctly follows this convention with no conditional guard. Note: Task 2 description body mentions '{{if ne .QueryAgeThresh ""}}' — this is an internal inconsistency in the spec text, but the SQL constant shown is authoritative and correct.

q3_reset_exists.detail: Collector.Reset() exists at internal/stat/stat.go:100-103. Currently clears prevPgStat/currPgStat only. Spec Task 5 correctly describes extending Reset() to also clear the four PID maps after they are added to Collector.

q4_collectstat_change_detection.detail: top/stat.go:81-85: ShowExtra change-detection calls c.ToggleCollectExtra(extra) and continues — it does NOT call c.Reset(). c.Reset() is called at lines 87-94 in the view-switch branch (when any new view arrives on viewCh). The spec's CollectExtra change-detection (trigger c.Reset() when CollectExtra changes) is architecturally sound: it adds a parallel branch in the same select that tracks v.CollectExtra changes separately from ShowExtra, because CollectExtra is read directly from view.View in Collector.Update() while ShowExtra goes through ToggleCollectExtra/c.config.collectExtra.

q5_iota_value.detail: internal/stat/stat.go:25-29 shows CollectNone=iota(0), CollectDiskstats(1), CollectNetdev(2), CollectFsstats(3), CollectLogtail(4). CollectProcPidStat=5 is the correct next value. No collision.
